events.title: Iran and US Exchange Strikes Across Middle East
events.desc: The United States and Iran engaged in reciprocal strikes across the Middle East for the second consecutive day. Iran reported retaliating against US assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain, with Bahrain confirming damage to its capital city overnight. This escalation follows a period of heightened tensions and direct strikes between the two nations.

events.title: US Strikes Oil Tanker, Killing Indian Sailors
events.desc: The US military attacked a Palau-flagged oil tanker, killing three Indian sailors. The US military claimed the tanker failed to comply with directions. This incident adds to the rising tensions and kinetic exchanges in the region.

events.title: US Strikes Iran in Response to Helicopter Downing
events.desc: President Donald Trump accused Iran of shooting down a US military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed retaliation. Subsequently, US forces carried out strikes against Iran in response.

events.title: Israeli Air Strikes Hit Lebanese City of Tyre
events.desc: Despite an Iranian warning to cease attacks on its ally Hezbollah, Israeli air strikes hit the Lebanese city of Tyre. Iran had previously warned Israel that resuming hostilities could lead to renewed conflict.

events.title: Israeli Strikes on Beirut Suburb Following Truce
events.desc: Israel conducted strikes on a Beirut suburb days after a US-brokered truce. The Israeli military stated the attacks were in response to Hezbollah firing into Israeli territory, indicating the fragile nature of the ceasefire.

events.title: US and Iran Signal Peace Deal is Close
events.desc: The United States and Iran have indicated that an agreement to end their war is imminent. A senior US administration official stated that both sides have agreed on a text and anticipate signing an initial deal in the coming days. Pakistan's Prime Minister also confirmed a final text has been agreed.

events.title: UAE Agrees to Unlock Billions for Iran Amid Ceasefire Push
events.desc: Sources indicate the United Arab Emirates has agreed to release billions of dollars for Iran. This potential move follows weeks of Iranian attacks on the UAE during the US-Israeli conflict with Iran. Abu Dhabi has issued a denial of these reports.

events.title: Israeli and Palestinian Civil Society Groups Urge Two-State Solution Support
events.desc: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups met in Paris to urge international support for a two-state solution, warning that the "window of opportunity" is closing. They highlighted the challenges posed by the current Israeli government's opposition to a sovereign Palestinian state.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 17
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon resulted in 17 fatalities. Nine of these individuals were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

events.title: Russian Grain Smuggling Expansion to Libya Tracked
events.desc: An investigation tracked the bulk carrier Grumant, which loaded grain at the occupied Ukrainian Port of Feodosia before a two-month journey to the Port of Benghazi in Libya. This highlights ongoing efforts to circumvent sanctions and potentially divert resources.

events.title: Banned Russian Submunitions Found in Mali
events.desc: Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets were discovered in a village in northern Mali, shortly after the Malian military announced airstrikes. Mali is a state party to the convention banning such weapons, raising concerns about their use and proliferation.

events.title: Damage to Police Infrastructure in Iran
events.desc: Bellingcat identified at least 80 police stations or related infrastructure damaged or destroyed in the first three weeks of the conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's "repressive capacity".

events.title: Iran and Israel Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Both Iran and Israel have stated they will pause strikes, with Israel's Prime Minister indicating a current ceasefire. Iran's armed forces also announced a halt to military actions. Both sides, however, issued warnings of retaliation if the ceasefire is breached again.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburb Days After US-Brokered Truce
events.desc: Israel conducted strikes in a suburb of Beirut, the capital of Lebanon, just days after a US-brokered truce was established. The Israeli military claimed the attacks were a direct response to Hezbollah firing at Israeli territory.

events.title: US Deportation Flight Carrying Iranians Lands in Central African Republic
events.desc: A US deportation flight carrying nationals from Iran, Afghanistan, Turkey, and Georgia landed in the Central African Republic (CAR). This action is part of the Trump administration's immigration policies, deporting individuals to third countries with which they have no connection, despite US State Department advisories warning against travel to CAR.
